title: Explosion in Monaco targets Ukrainian oligarch Vadim Ermolaev
subhead: The homemade bomb that exploded on Monday evening in front of a building in the principality apparently targeted Ermolaev, a wealthy Ukrainian businessman. Although he had renounced his nationality, he still maintained opaque business activities connected to his country of origin.

Spain has three months to review one million applications for migrant regularization
subhead: Prime Minister Pedro Sanchez's government is defending a measure aimed at supporting economic growth amid a 'demographic winter.' This is Spain's seventh such initiative in 40 years. Initially, the plan was met with broad consensus, but it has since become a source of division.

title: Global oceans experience record heat in June, driven by El Niño and climate change
subhead: The average surface temperature reached 20.98°C in June, surpassing the previous 2024 high, according to the European observatory Copernicus Marine. The first half of 2026 was the second-warmest on record.

title: Mbappé fires France into World Cup last 16
subhead: France's victory over Sweden on Tuesday has elevated prolific striker Kylian Mbappé to an incredible World Cup record:  18 in 18 matches – only one behind Argentina's Lionel Messi's all-time record of 19 goals.

title: EU judges' opaque handling of potential conflicts of interest
subhead: Members of the Court of Justice of the European Union have ruled on cases involving companies or economic sectors in which they had interests, according to an investigation conducted since 2024 by Investigate Europe and its partners, including Le Monde.

The killing of West Bank children by Israel's army, a product of Palestinians' dehumanization
subhead: The Israeli human rights organization B'Tselem documented the deaths of 54 minors killed by the Israeli army in the occupied West Bank in January 2025 and the impunity enjoyed by its soldiers.

title: 'Their mouths, eyes, hands, were taped and they were thrown into the hole': Russian soldiers confront Putin with reality of front lines
subhead: A Russian exile media outlet has analyzed nearly 7,000 letters from Russian soldiers and their families to the Kremlin thanks to a security flaw.

title: 'The King and the Mockingbird': The animated masterpiece that inspired Studio Ghibli
subhead: His film took more than three decades to reach the screen. In 1946, Paul Grimault, then 41, began work on 'Le Roi et l'Oiseau' ('The King and the Mockingbird') with Jacques Prévert writing the screenplay. He finally saw it screened in 1979, at 74. The hand-drawn animation became a cult classic, inspiring generations of viewers and filmmakers.
